message:
feat(core): deterministic reindex/backfill tooling with integrity checks (wa-oegrb.4.4)

Adds ReindexPipeline for full rebuild and partial backfill of the Tantivy
lexical index from the recorder source-of-truth log. Supports resumable
checkpoints with separate consumer IDs, ordinal and time-range backfill,
and IntegrityChecker for verifying index consistency against the log.
32 tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1034,7 +1034,7 @@
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.4.1","title":"Design versioned Tantivy schema for recorder text + metadata filters","description":"## Context
Lexical retrieval quality and index stability depend on getting schema decisions right early (field structure, analyzers, stored vs indexed fields, offset references, metadata filters).

## Scope
Design the canonical Tantivy schema for recorder events, including text fields, normalized metadata, and time/agent/pane filters.

## Deliverables
- Versioned Tantivy schema definition with migration notes
- Field-by-field rationale (why indexed/stored/tokenized)
- Analyzer/tokenizer strategy tuned for terminal text (commands, stack traces, mixed symbols)
- Golden tests for schema compatibility and queryability

## Why this matters
Schema drift or poor tokenization destroys search quality and makes reindexing expensive.

## Acceptance
- Schema supports required filters and retrieval use-cases
- Analyzer choices are justified with examples
- Versioning strategy is documented for future migrations","notes":"Delivered Tantivy lexical schema contract in docs/flight-recorder/tantivy-schema-v1.md, including field-by-field schema, tokenizer strategy for terminal text/symbols, migration/rebuild semantics, and golden compatibility/queryability test plan. Linked contract from ADR and cross-project extraction matrix for downstream implementation beads.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleSnow","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.46742Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T03:29:53.243622-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T03:27:34.625042-05:00","labels":["architecture","epic","flight-recorder","tantivy","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.46742Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.826595Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.855245Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.883639Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","title":"Implement incremental Tantivy ingestion from append-log checkpoints","description":"## Context
Recorder writes are append-only; indexer must consume offsets incrementally with deterministic resume behavior and bounded lag.

## Scope
Implement incremental index ingestion from recorder checkpoints, including idempotent retries and commit cadence controls.

## Deliverables
- Offset-driven incremental indexer pipeline
- Checkpoint coordination with storage replay cursors
- Commit/flush policy for freshness vs throughput tradeoffs
- Integration tests for restart/resume without data loss

## Why this matters
Incremental indexing is the bridge between high-throughput logging and usable near-real-time search.

## Acceptance
- Indexer resumes deterministically after restarts
- No silent drops/duplication under normal retry paths
- Lag is measurable and bounded under target load","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.499041Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T16:58:55.057711-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T16:58:55.057711-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented incremental Tantivy ingestion pipeline: AppendLogReader (binary log scanning, seek-to-ordinal, torn-tail safe), IndexDocumentFields (all 24 schema fields), map_event_to_document for all 4 event types with redaction handling, IndexWriter trait for pluggable backends, IncrementalIndexer with checkpoint-coordinated batching/resume/dedup, IndexerLagMonitor. 40 tests covering cold start, resume from checkpoint, multi-pane, mixed types, schema mismatch skip, commit failure propagation, lag monitoring.","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","implementation","tantivy","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.499041Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.652992Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.911617Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.4.3","title":"Tune Tantivy commit/merge policy for sustained recorder ingest and query latency","description":"## Context
Tantivy segment management strongly affects write amplification and query latency. We need explicit merge and commit strategies aligned with recorder workload.

## Scope
Tune and codify segment lifecycle policy (commit frequency, merge heuristics, memory budgets) for terminal transcript data.

## Deliverables
- Merge policy configuration and rationale
- Benchmarked commit/merge profiles for common load regimes
- Operational toggles for safe runtime tuning
- Tests/benchmarks guarding against pathological segment explosion

## Why this matters
Poor segment strategy can make a correct index unusably slow or expensive.

## Acceptance
- Query latency and ingest throughput remain within target envelopes
- Segment growth remains bounded/predictable
- Policy is tunable without code surgery","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.530604Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T16:37:07.222087-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T16:14:43.246326-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in tantivy_policy.rs: LoadRegime, CommitPolicy, MergePolicyConfig, MergePolicyEvaluator, CommitTracker, AdaptivePolicyController. 45 tests pass.","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","performance","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.530604Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.681399Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"wa-oegrb.4.4","title":"Implement deterministic reindex/backfill tooling from recorder source-of-truth log","description":"## Context
We need deterministic rebuild/backfill tooling for schema evolution, corruption recovery, and historical imports.

## Scope
Create tooling to rebuild lexical index from recorder log ranges, with progress reporting and safety checks.

## Deliverables
- Full reindex command path with resumable checkpoints
- Partial/range backfill support by time/offset
- Integrity checks comparing index docs to source offsets
- Operator runbook for rebuild scenarios

## Why this matters
Without robust rebuild tooling, index incidents become high-risk and long-duration.

## Acceptance
- Rebuild can be run safely on large datasets
- Progress and completion criteria are explicit
- Integrity validation detects missing/corrupt ranges","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.562741Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.711435Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","operations","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.562741Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.711388Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
+{"id":"wa-oegrb.4.4","title":"Implement deterministic reindex/backfill tooling from recorder source-of-truth log","description":"## Context
We need deterministic rebuild/backfill tooling for schema evolution, corruption recovery, and historical imports.

## Scope
Create tooling to rebuild lexical index from recorder log ranges, with progress reporting and safety checks.

## Deliverables
- Full reindex command path with resumable checkpoints
- Partial/range backfill support by time/offset
- Integrity checks comparing index docs to source offsets
- Operator runbook for rebuild scenarios

## Why this matters
Without robust rebuild tooling, index incidents become high-risk and long-duration.

## Acceptance
- Rebuild can be run safely on large datasets
- Progress and completion criteria are explicit
- Integrity validation detects missing/corrupt ranges","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.562741Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T17:28:15.761828-05:00","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","operations","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.562741Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.711388Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","title":"Build lexical query service over Tantivy with stable filters/ranking/snippets","description":"## Context
Hybrid search still depends on high-quality lexical retrieval as both baseline and fallback. Query behavior must be explicit, testable, and stable.

## Scope
Implement lexical query service (filters, ranking, snippets/highlights, pagination) over the Tantivy index.

## Deliverables
- Query API for lexical search with deterministic sorting/pagination
- Filter support (time, pane, session, agent, direction, tags)
- Highlight/snippet strategy suitable for terminal text
- Relevance regression tests and fixture corpus

## Why this matters
Lexical search is the reliability anchor; semantic rankers should enhance, not replace, this foundation.

## Acceptance
- Lexical results are accurate and predictable
- Filters and pagination semantics are stable
- Fixture-based tests protect behavior from regressions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.592975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T17:26:12.331028-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T17:26:12.331028-05:00","close_reason":"Lexical query service implemented: SearchQuery builder, 17 filter types, cursor pagination, TF scoring with field boosts, snippet extraction, InMemorySearchService reference impl. 47 tests.","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","query","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.592975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.740105Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.768214Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.4.6","title":"Create lexical quality harness with golden queries, relevance assertions, and latency budgets","description":"## Context
Search correctness can silently degrade with tokenizer, schema, or ranking changes. We need explicit evaluation harnesses tied to expected outcomes.

## Scope
Establish lexical search quality harness (golden query set + relevance assertions + latency checks).

## Deliverables
- Golden query dataset derived from realistic terminal workflows
- Relevance assertions (must-hit docs, ordering tolerances)
- Latency budget checks per query class
- CI wiring for regression detection

## Why this matters
A formal harness converts subjective “search feels off” complaints into actionable, reproducible failures.

## Acceptance
- Harness runs in CI/local and fails on quality regressions
- Query corpus covers critical forensic/operator scenarios
- Metrics are baseline-tracked for future improvements","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.623998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.796985Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","testing","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.623998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.796937Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.5","title":"Semantic track: embeddings + vector retrieval + hybrid rank fusion","description":"## Track objective
Implement semantic retrieval and hybrid lexical+semantic ranking over recorder data, reusing validated patterns from cass/xf where appropriate.

## Why this track exists
Pure lexical search misses semantically similar incidents and prior solutions. Hybrid ranking materially improves operator and agent recall in incident/debug workflows.

## Deliverables
- Embedding/chunking strategy for recorder corpus
- Vector retrieval subsystem and ANN strategy
- Rank-fusion strategy combining lexical and semantic evidence
- Offline relevance evaluation corpus and metrics

## Acceptance criteria
- Hybrid ranking behavior is deterministic enough to test and tune.
- Semantic subsystem can degrade gracefully without breaking lexical search.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.478129Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.792358Z","labels":["cass","hybrid","search","semantic","xf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.478129Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.792305Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
